title: Mustardy Crisp Algerian Saskatoon Berries Tagine
description: A tangy, one-pot Algerian dish featuring Saskatoon berries and crispy mustard topping. The combination of sweet and savory flavors will make this dish a new favorite.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the coriander seeds, cumin seeds, salt, and black pepper, and stir for 1 minute.
Add the saskatoon berries, lemon juice, and 1 cup of water, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the oven.
Bake at 375°F for 20-25 minutes, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
While the tagine is cooking, mix the mustard seeds with a pinch of salt in a small bowl.
After the tagine has finished cooking, remove it from the oven and sprinkle the mustard-seed mixture on top.
Return the pot to the stovetop and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mustard seeds are crispy and golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.
Note: If using frozen saskatoon berries, thaw them first and pat dry with a paper towel before using.
